_meta.notes: Translate values only. NEVER translate: Vietnamese example words (đêm, dấu, việt…), Telex key sequences (daaus, vieetj, aa, ow…), the words 'Telex', 'VietTelex', 'English / ABC', emoji, HTML tags like <b>. Vietnamese tone names (sắc, huyền, hỏi, ngã, nặng) stay Vietnamese — add a short gloss in the target language in parentheses if natural. Keep the friendly, kid-appropriate tone.

ui.imeTitle: Turn OFF your Vietnamese IME first
ui.imeBody:
Lessons compose the diacritics for you from plain keystrokes, so switch your system keyboard to English / ABC (turn off VietTelex, Unikey, EVKey…) before practicing.

💡 On a Mac: press 🌐 or ⌃Space to switch input sources. With an IME on, letters get marked TWICE and the lesson can't match your keys.
ui.imeToast: ⚠️ Your system Vietnamese IME seems to be ON — switch to the English/ABC keyboard so lessons see your real keys.
ui.noVoice: No Vietnamese voice on this device 😢
ui.infoDone: 📖 +10 XP — hand-position guide read, next lesson unlocked!
ui.trackTitle: Where do you want to start?
ui.trackNewD: Start with hand position and touch typing, then learn Telex.
ui.trackTypistT:
I can type —
just teach me Telex
ui.trackTypistD: Jump straight to special letters and tones. The first two chapters stay open for review.
ui.loadFail: Could not load lessons — open the page via

chapters.c2: Special letters: â ê ô ă ơ ư đ

c0l2: Put your index fingers on F and J (feel the bumps). One finger per key, then return home.
c0l3: Reach up, then RETURN to the home row. Eyes on the screen, not the keys.
c0l4: Vietnamese uses V, N, M, C a lot. Slow and accurate first — speed comes later.
c1l1: Real Vietnamese words, no diacritics yet. Press SPACE to move to the next word.
c1l2: Still no diacritics — build muscle memory between keys.
c1l3: A full sentence! Type each word, separated by spaces.
c2l1: Type d TWICE to get đ. Watch the letter transform as you type!
c2l2: Type a twice to get â. Example: caan → cân.
c2l3: Same rule: double the vowel. teen → tên, coo → cô.
c2l4: The w key: ă ơ ư
w is the magic key: aw → ă, ow → ơ, uw → ư, and w alone → ư.
c2l5: One word can need several transforms: thuwowng → thương (ư then ơ).
c3l1: Finish the word, then press s for the rising tone: mas → má.
c3l2: The f key adds the falling tone: maf → mà.
c3l3: The r key adds the asking tone: har → hả.
c3l4: The x key adds the tumbling tone: max → mã.
c3l5: The j key adds the heavy tone: maj → mạ.
c3l6: Type a DIFFERENT tone key to change it (más + f → mà); press z to remove.
c3l7: The full set: ma má mà mả mã mạ — try the 🔊 button on each!
c4l1: Our country's name! Capitals: hold Shift and type normally.
c4l2: Type the whole sentence — press 🔊 to hear it first.
c4l4: Vietnamese folk verse — type and learn the language at once.
c5l1: Vowel marks go RIGHT AFTER the vowel, tones at the end: daaus → dấu.
c5l2: Full sentences with punctuation and capitals.
c5l3: The final challenge — every special letter, all five tones.
c6l1: The legend of the Vietnamese origin — type it sentence by sentence.
c6l2: Why square banh chung at Tet?
c6l3: King Le and the golden turtle in Hanoi.
c7l1: The boy of Giong village who grew into a warrior.
c7l3: The final boss: type the whole Tet story!

hands.lead: Before the lessons, place your hands right: each finger owns a few keys and returns to the home row. This is the foundation for everything below.
notes: 1️⃣ Find the F and J bumps
Feel the keyboard: F and J have small ridges. Rest your index fingers there — no looking! The other fingers sit on A S D (left) and K L ; (right); thumbs hover over the space bar.
The lesson keyboard is coloured by finger: each finger presses its own keys and returns home. Being slow at first is normal — your hands will remember.
Try not to look down. Lessons always highlight the next key and name the finger, so you never hunt for keys by sight.
Straight back, elbows ~90°, wrists straight and not pressed onto the desk. Rest 5 minutes after every 20.

hoc.lead: 8 chapters, from meeting the keyboard to typing history tales. Earn at least ⭐ on a lesson to unlock the next. On a tablet without a keyboard, tap the on-screen keys inside each lesson.

landing.heroSub: Type without diacritics, get full Vietnamese. Instant feedback — the exact rule set of the ViệtTelex input method.
landing.sandboxLabel: ✏️ Try it now — type Vietnamese the Telex way
landing.kbdNote: 💡 This box needs a real keyboard. Press space to end a word. The classroom has an on-screen keyboard — tablets work too.
landing.hocLead:
An 8-chapter course: hand position → special letters → tones → full stories.
Stars ⭐, badges 🏅, spoken examples 🔊 — and an on-screen keyboard for tablets.
landing.ctaNote: Free, no account — progress is saved on your device.
landing.cheatLead: Click a tile to see it run in the try-box above. Remember these few groups and you can type all of Vietnamese.

groups.Dấu thanh (gõ ở cuối từ): Tones (typed at the end of the word)
